home.heroSubtitle: From your home country to settled life in Korea — visa, home, job and taxes, in your language.
home.countryHint: Start from your home country — we'll guide you from the visa application onward.
home.why1Body: A roadmap tailored to your country and visa — starting before you even arrive.
home.why2Body: Understand deposits and leases, and connect with licensed agents.
home.why3Body: Year-end settlement and refunds, then connect with a tax accountant.
home.freeNote: Free to use. WelKor provides information and connects you to verified licensed professionals.

select.title: Why are you coming to Korea?
select.hint: Pick the situation that fits you — you don't need to know any visa names. We'll show the right visa and your step-by-step path.
select.notSureTitle: Not sure which one fits you?
select.notSureBody: Check the official visa navigator, or ask people who've done it in the community.

explore.title: Explore Korea
explore.subtitle: Most visitors don't need a visa for a short trip. Here's where to go, and where to book — straight from official and trusted sources.
explore.noVisaTitle: Do you need a visa for tourism?
explore.noVisaBody: Many nationalities enter visa-free for up to 90 days with a K-ETA travel authorization; others use a C-3 short-term visa. Check your country on the official K-ETA site before you book.
explore.bookingNote: WelKor links you to established platforms — we don't sell travel ourselves.
explore.disclaimer: WelKor provides travel information and links to third-party platforms (Trip.com, Airbnb, Klook and others) and official sources (VisitKorea, K-ETA). We are not a travel agency and earn no booking commission at this stage. Always verify entry rules on the official K-ETA site for your nationality.

journey.notFound: We don't have a roadmap for that visa yet.

housing.title: Housing
housing.subtitle: Plan your budget, then browse the major portals on a map. WelKor shows information only — we are not a licensed agent and never broker or take commission.
housing.priceNote: Rough 2025 references for a one-room (원룸), monthly rent. Amounts in 만원 (1 만원 = about ₩10,000). Always confirm on the portals.
housing.portalsNote: Search by neighborhood on Korea's main listing portals. A live in-app map is coming.
housing.sampleNote: Examples only — to calibrate your expectations. Live, verified listings come with licensed-agent onboarding.
housing.shortStayNote: For short trips before you settle, book a hotel or stay through these platforms.
housing.guide1: Move-in registration (전입신고) + fixed date (확정일자) protect your deposit.
housing.guide2: Check the property register (등기부등본) for the owner and any loans.
housing.guide3: Jeonse = large refundable deposit, no rent. Wolse = smaller deposit + monthly rent.
housing.disclaimer: WelKor is not a licensed real-estate agent (공인중개사). Price ranges are approximate references, and external listings belong to third-party portals (Naver, Zigbang, Dabang, Ziptoss). Verify every property and contract with a licensed agent before paying any deposit.

finance.title: Banking & finance
finance.subtitle: How foreigners open accounts, get cards, borrow and send money home. WelKor shows information only — we are not a bank or financial adviser and earn nothing from these links.
finance.officialIntro: For rules and to avoid illegal lending, check the official regulator.
finance.disclaimer: WelKor is not a financial institution, lender or adviser, and provides no financial product. This is general information; eligibility, fees and rates are set by each provider and must be confirmed directly. Use only licensed institutions.

study.title: Study in Korea
study.subtitle: Choose a field and a university, see what each admissions office needs, and prepare your student-visa documents. WelKor shows information and official links only — we are not an education agency and never file applications for you.
study.startBody: “Study in Korea” is the government portal listing accredited universities, programs and scholarships for international students. Use it to verify any school or program.
study.fieldsNote: A quick map of where international students usually go — most top schools offer programs in each.
study.schoolsNote: Real universities with their official English sites. “Find admissions” opens a live search so you always reach the current page.
study.gksBody: The Global Korea Scholarship (GKS) funds tuition and living costs for international students. Many programs also ask for a TOPIK Korean-proficiency level — plan for it early.
study.docsNote: A general checklist — the exact list depends on your nationality and the Korean mission. Always confirm before you apply.
study.disclaimer: WelKor provides study information and links to official sources (Study in Korea, GKS, TOPIK, HiKorea) and university sites. We are not an education or visa agency, do not file applications, and earn nothing from these links. University details and visa requirements are set by each institution and the Korean government — verify everything directly before applying or paying.

jobs.title: Find work in Korea — matched to your visa
jobs.subtitle: Pick your visa and the field you want, and we lay out what you can legally do, the channels that fit you, and official information — all at once. WelKor provides information only; you apply directly on each external site.
jobs.noticeBody: WelKor provides job information only. We do not place, broker, or represent candidates, and we do not guarantee employment. Apply directly on each external site yourself.
jobs.visaUnknown: I don't know my visa yet / I don't have one
jobs.fieldQuestion: What kind of work are you looking for?
jobs.block1Title: What your visa legally lets you do
jobs.block1Empty: Choose your visa above and we'll show the work it allows and the working conditions.
jobs.block2Hint: Each link opens a search filtered to your field and language. Foreigner-friendly channels are shown first.
jobs.hikoreaScope: Check the exact work scope for your visa (HiKorea)
jobs.leaveNotice: Following a link takes you to an external job site. Applications, contact and contracts happen directly on that site; WelKor is not involved.
jobs.epsBody: E-9 (non-professional) employment is handled only through the government Employment Permit System (EPS). Private brokering is prohibited by law. WelKor links to official EPS information only.
jobs.disclaimer: WelKor provides job information only. We do not place, broker, or represent candidates, do not match candidates, charge fees, handle applications, connect EPS (E-9) employment, or guarantee any job or outcome. All listings link to third-party or government platforms; eligibility and hiring are decided there. Always confirm that a job matches your visa status.

tax.title: Tax
tax.subtitle: Guides only — filing is done by a licensed tax accountant (세무사) you connect with here.
tax.disclaimer: This is general information, not tax advice. Verify your case with a licensed professional.

community.title: Community
community.subtitle: Questions and tips from people who settled before you.

footer.disclaimer: WelKor is an information and connection platform. It is not a licensed real-estate, tax or administrative agent. Service connections are provided by verified licensed professionals.
